what_it_does: A build-phase implementation guide for the 'substrate unification' loop in the one-application stream: it maps criteria groups to build phases, prescribes an ordered sequence of B-* build criteria, T-* Tim decisions, and R-* reconcile gates, states dos/don'ts each with a because, and records standing laws and ledger-conformance fixes that must not regress.
observations: It is doc 2 of 3 in the loop-prep set, paired with UNION-COMPLETION-CRITERIA and UNION-RESEARCH-SYNTHESIS.
It organizes work by build phase (PHASE 0 foundations, then groups for IDENTITY, REG, SEAM, STORE, ACT, LENS, EDGE, INST, LEGIBILITY, TH, COV, RECONCILE) rather than by criteria concept.

It repeatedly corrects a prior draft: e.g., `_RESOLVABLE` is a drift-guard not a bug; `SCHEMES` must be derived from a file-discovered `scheme/` registry; the recursive `vec://cluster://` grammar must land before flattening `SCHEMES`; lifters are producers, not predicates.
It enforces a 'unions-not-bridges / absorb, don't connect' law: one mechanism, one source, one event log, one scope base, one disposition system, one projection engine.
It distinguishes three decision tiers: B-* build criteria (lead decides), T-* genuine meaning calls (Tim decides), and R-* sibling-stream reconciles (lead/fabric-owned, not Tim).
It includes a LEDGER-CONFORMANCE block listing specific prior fixes (F1–F5, STORE1b/4b/7, honest-status split, tree-sitter, RCN7, lead-decision artifacts) that this build must not regress.

Standing laws include: registry-is-truth/no-hardcoding, additive changes, fail-loud, reuse-don't-parallel, the floor (pure read, no dispatch by construction), ext4 storage, Rule-7 confirm-before-mutation, operator-gated build wire, C9.4 three-anchor + self-description, FORM is half of done, honest status buckets, verify-by-use, no feature branches.
It declares file territory: some new files are parallel lanes; hot files must be serialized and claimed in WORK-SPLIT.md; some files are never touched by this loop.
It requires every 'lead decision recorded' to exist as a durable artifact (channel synthesis or registry/decision row); a prose tag alone is treated as not-yet-decided.
It uses epistemic labels (Observed / Inferred / Verified) and insists they be preserved, not laundered into present-tense fact.
standouts: The aggressive 'absorb, don't connect' framing and the explicit rejection of bridge solutions.
The detailed correction of the prior draft's mislabels, especially the `_RESOLVABLE`-is-not-a-bug and derive-`SCHEMES`-from-registry flips.
The strict separation of lead decisions from Tim decisions, with a rule that developer/architecture calls must never be routed to Tim.
The requirement that every build criterion be verified by use against real data, not just code-read.
conventions: Uses B-*/T-*/R-* prefixed identifiers to classify work items.
Each 'Do' and 'Don't' is paired with a 'because' clause.
Status is reported with four emoji buckets: ✅ Verified, 🟡 Designed, 🟠 Built-unexercised, 🔴 Gap/Broken.
Claims carry epistemic labels: Observed, Inferred, Verified.
References external files by relative path and line numbers, but does not duplicate their content.
No feature branches in `~/company`; commit to main.
concerns: The document is extremely dense and self-referential; a build-worker could miss dependencies or ordering gates.
Many decisions are tagged as 'recorded' but the file itself warns that if the durable artifact is missing the decision is not yet made, creating a large verification burden.

The file is truncated in the provided excerpt, so some later sections (e.g., full storage migration, ACT, LENS, EDGE, INST, LEGIBILITY, TH, COV, RECONCILE, DECISIONS-FOR-TIM) are incomplete or absent from the visible text.
The sheer volume of standing laws and ledger-conformance items may make it hard to keep all of them load-bearing during implementation.
notes: A highly normative, self-correcting build rulebook that tries to prevent both parallel-mechanism sprawl and false routing of developer decisions to Tim.
questions: What exactly is 'the loop' and how does it iterate over these docs?
What are the concrete contents of the companion files it references, such as `UNION-COMPLETION-CRITERIA.md` and `UNION-RESEARCH-SYNTHESIS.md`?
What do terms like `SCHEMES`, `_RESOLVABLE`, `RULE_OPS`, `cc_launch`, and `mcp_face` mean in this codebase?
Which durable artifacts already exist for the 'recorded' lead decisions, and which are still pending?
What is the full content of the truncated later sections (storage migration, ACT, LENS, EDGE, INST, etc.)?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is a build sequence, dos/don'ts, and file map; the actual content is also a governance and correction document that enforces decision ownership, epistemic honesty, and anti-regression rules.
apparent_themes: Substrate unification through absorption, not bridging.
Fail-loud governance and honest status reporting.
Clear ownership of decisions: lead vs Tim vs fabric reconcile.
Additive, non-breaking evolution of shared infrastructure.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to stop the build from re-creating parallel mechanisms, from regressing recently fixed divergences, and from parking decidable engineering work behind false 'needs Tim' gates, while still reserving genuine meaning questions for Tim.
novelty: The document embeds an unusually explicit meta-governance layer: epistemic labels on every claim, a divergence ledger conformance block, a 'both-plus-others' decision heuristic, and a hard rule that prose tags are not recorded decisions.
contribution: A structured, law-governed implementation plan with explicit build ordering, decision ownership, and regression guards that a fused system would keep as the canonical rule set for this build loop.
summary_for_embedding: A build-phase implementation guide that orders substrate-unification work into B-* criteria, T-* Tim decisions, and R-* reconcile gates, under 'absorb, don't connect' laws and ledger-conformance fixes.
intention_for_embedding: To give build workers a corrected, decision-owned, fail-loud HOW for unifying code/design substrates without regressing prior fixes or creating parallel mechanisms.
